footer.tagline: Self-hostable fingerprint browser. We sell software, not data hosting — your data lives only on your device.

hero.eyebrow: v0.1.0 RC · Self-hostable · No data retention
hero.title: Trust nothing. Verify everything.
hero.subtitle: Veil is a fingerprint browser for high-value multi-account, Web3, e-commerce, and automation teams. Your data stays on your device, and the software is self-hostable — we sell the software, not data hosting.
hero.description: Veil is a fingerprint browser for high-value multi-account, Web3, e-commerce, and automation teams. Your data stays on your device, and the software is self-hostable — we sell the software, not data hosting.

features.title: Replace "trust the platform" with "verify the system"
features.description: Six core features that set Veil apart from other fingerprint browsers.

useCases.title: Built for high-value identity isolation
useCases.description: Multi-account, wallet isolation, storefront operations, automation teams — Veil turns "isolation" into a verifiable engineering concern.

roadmap.title: From single-device complete to desktop source release

pricingPreview.title: Three tiers, identical features
pricingPreview.description: Only duration and average monthly cost differ. Every tier ships with unlimited profiles, self-hosting, and zero data retention on our side.

finalCta.title: You don't need to trust us.
finalCta.body: You just need the ability to verify us. Veil runs self-hosted — there is nothing for an attacker to take from us. It's design, not a promise.

gallery.title: Turn invisible security into pointable details
gallery.description: Three visual slices of the core mechanisms — fingerprint consistency, outbound connection audit, and hard isolation between profiles. Each one corresponds to a promise the code can verify.

tiers.title: Choose your subscription duration
tiers.description: Identical feature set across all tiers — only the duration and average monthly cost differ. Every update and new release is included during the subscription period.
tiers.footnote: * Full refund within 7 days if not activated. Expired subscriptions don't lock local data, but stop receiving updates and patches.

faq.title: Before you decide
faq.description: If your question isn't covered below, please email hi@useveil.xyz.

compare.title: How Veil differs from comparable tools
compare.description: We only list independently verifiable facts. Pricing reflects public pages as of May 2026.
compare.footnote: * Pricing and feature sets may change. Confirm vendor websites before purchasing.

download.title: Choose your platform
download.description: After purchasing a license, download the installer for your platform here. Every release ships with SHA-256 checksums.

verification.title: How to verify your download
verification.body: Compute the hash with shasum -a 256 FILENAME and compare to the SHA-256 above to ensure the file hasn't been tampered with.

useCases.title: Different audiences, same job: verifiable isolation
useCases.description: Four real workflow scenarios. In each one, Veil cares not about looking secure, but about being provably secure.

automation.title: Profiles as callable resources
automation.description: Veil exposes a local HTTP API (lifecycle management) and standard CDP endpoints (page operations). Playwright, Puppeteer, and Selenium all connect directly.

channels.title: Two endpoints, two responsibilities
channels.description: HTTP API handles lifecycle. CDP handles page behavior. Separate auth, separate ports, zero contamination between them.

quickStart.title: Three steps from zero to a scriptable profile
quickStart.description: Generate an API key in the desktop app, start a profile over HTTP, connect via any CDP framework. The master password is never involved.

code.title: Pick your framework — drop in the snippet
code.description: Switch tabs to see the shortest working snippet per framework. All snippets are end-to-end runnable; copy and ship.

auth.title: API keys are fully decoupled from the master password
auth.body: Automation access is separated from human access by design. API keys live independently, scope-limited, and revocable on demand — they never touch the master password or recovery phrase.

changelog.title: Every release, recorded
changelog.description: What's new, what changed, what was fixed, known limits, and upgrade paths.

stack.title: From master password to ciphertext on disk
stack.description: Each layer uses industry-standard algorithms and is independently replaceable. Compromising one layer doesn't grant access to the next.

trust.title: Four specific promises, each one verifiable

threats.title: What Veil protects against — and what it doesn't
threats.description: We honestly list both the threats we cover and the ones we don't. No security product is effective against attacks outside its threat model.
